title: Configuration Change Authorization Linkage for Network Devices

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
spl:
index=network sourcetype=pan:config earliest=-7d
| rex field=cmd "(?i)CHG(?<chg>[0-9]{6,10})"
| lookup esp_device_inventory.csv hostname AS host OUTPUT in_scope
| where in_scope="true"
| join type=left chg [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=chg:network earliest=-30d
    | rename number as chg
    | stats latest(state) as ticket_state by chg
  ]
| where isnull(ticket_state) OR ticket_state!="Closed"
| table _time, host, cmd, chg, ticket_state
value: We require network configuration commits on ESP devices to reference an approved change identifier in syslog or commit comments.
implementation: (1) Standardize change number format in commit templates. (2) Map devices to ESP scope. (3) Daily alert on missing tickets. (4) Escalate repeat violators.
visualization: Table (missing linkage), Bar chart (by device), Single value (count)
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t latest(All_Changes.status) as agg_value from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - agg_value

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks

knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
